text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 17 November 2021 

Dr Amelia Patel  
St. George’s University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Department of Neurology, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Amelia Patel,  

Alex Turner, DOB: 14-03-1992, Hospital No: H928374 NHS No. 9038475621  
52 Meadow Lane, Croydon, CR0 7PA 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent seizures of uncertain classification; workup ongoing. 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily 
Previously tried: Carbamazepine (discontinued due to dizziness and rash) 

Plan: 

• Continue Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily 
• Maintain diary of mood/irritability prodrome and events; bring to next review 
• Safety advice discussed including bathing precautions and sleep optimisation 
• Arrange repeat EEG and MRI brain with epilepsy protocol; check serum levels and vitamin D 
• Follow up in 4 months or sooner if events escalate 

I reviewed Alex Turner in clinic today. They report brief warning symptoms of low mood and irritability on some days, which they have been documenting in a diary. These symptoms occasionally precede their events by several hours. Despite good adherence to Levetiracetam, they continue to have seizures every 4 weeks. The most recent event occurred 10 days ago without injury or prolonged postictal confusion. No clear nocturnal predominance and no consistent motor pattern described; there is no reliable eyewitness account to further characterise the events at present. Sleep deprivation and high stress at work may act as triggers. 

Neurological examination today was unremarkable. We discussed the importance of continuing the diary, particularly noting any prodromal mood/irritability, potential triggers, and recovery times, to aid classification and treatment planning. Given tolerability of current therapy, I have advised continuing Levetiracetam at the present dose while we complete further investigations. If the frequency persists, we will consider dose optimisation or an alternative agent at review. Driving regulations were discussed in line with DVLA guidance, and the patient understands their responsibilities. 

I have not arranged a routine interim phone call but would be happy to be contacted via the clinic if there is any clinical deterioration or if events become more frequent than every 4 weeks. 

With best wishes,

entities.text: they continue to have seizures every 4 weeks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "they continue to have seizures every 4 weeks." This provides a clear numeric frequency. No other conflicting frequencies are mentioned. Therefore, normalize to the allowed format as 1 per 4 week.
seizure_frequency_number: they continue to have seizures every 4 weeks
